definition: Ajka Crystal is a [Hungarian](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hungary) manufacturer of [crystal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lead_glass) created in 1878 by Bernard Neumann.

Alibi marketing and alibi branding are [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) and [branding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) strategies used by companies to circumvent restrictions on advertising certain classes of products by instead advertising an ==See also== * [Ambush marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ambush_marketing) * [Brand extension](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_extension) * [Guerilla marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Guerilla_marketing) ==References== [[Category:Marketing]] [[Category:Brand management]] [[Category:Alcohol advertising]] [[

definition: Andy Stefanovich (born 1966) is an American speaker, bestselling author, and business consultant.

Aneta Bogdan is a Romanian [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand)ing, [corporate identity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corporate_identity) and [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) consultant.

AntiGravity Fitness is a fitness company founded by [Christopher Calvin Harrison](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christopher_Calvin_Harrison) in 2007 and based in [New York City](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/New_York_City), specializing in hybrid aerial fitness techniques that combine [silk hammocks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aerial_silk) with [modern yoga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Modern_yoga), [pilates](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pilates), [barre exercises](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barre_(ballet)), and traditional [strength training](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strength_training) techniques for aerialists into different exercise curriculums.
Arc Holdings is the holding company of the Arc Group, specializing in the design and manufacturing of glass tableware.

aspirational brand is a term in consumer [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) for a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) or [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) which a large segment of its exposure audience wishes to own, but for economic reasons cannot.

categories: Branding companies of the United States

definition: Big Idea in [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) and [advertising](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Advertising) is a term used to describe the core [concept](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Concept) behind an [advertising campaign](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Advertising_campaign), a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand), or product to the [general public](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/General_public), by creating a strong message that resonates with the [consumer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer)s.

Bikram Yoga is a system of [hot yoga](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hot_yoga), a type of [yoga as exercise](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yoga_as_exercise), spread by [Bikram Choudhury](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bikram_Choudhury) and based on the teachings of [[B.
blind stamp ('blind' meaning uncoloured) is an image, design or lettering on an art print or book formed by creating a depression in the paper or other material.
Bloom Consulting is a [consulting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consulting) firm, specialised in [nation branding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nation_branding).

BodyAttack is a commercial group-fitness sports inspired cardio workout program including some sports-derived movements, aimed primarily at developing [cardiovascular fitness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cardiovascular_fitness).
BOSU Balance Trainer (or BOSU ball) is a fitness [training](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Training) device, invented in 1999 by David Weck.

media.file: Two people in a gym using BOSU balls.jpg
Half ab ball or BOSU Ball.webp

definition: Boxercise is a [high intensity interval training](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-intensity_interval_training) class based on [boxing training](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boxing_training).
Brand alliances is a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand)ing strategy used in a [business alliance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Business_alliance).

brand ambassador (sometimes also called a corporate ambassador) is a person paid by an [organization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organization) or [company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Company) to represent its [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) in a positive light, helping to increase [brand awareness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_awareness) and [sales](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sales).
brand architecture is the structure of [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand)s within an [organizational entity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organisation).
Brand aversion is an antonym of [brand loyalty](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_loyalty).
Brand awareness is the extent to which customers are able to recall or recognize a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) under different conditions.
brand blunder is an error associated with the branding of a product, especially a new product in a new market.
Brand engagement is the process of forming an emotional or rational attachment between a [consumer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer) and a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand).

Brand equity, in [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing), is the worth of a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) in and of itself – i.e., the social value of a well-known [brand name](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_name).

Brand EU Centre (in short, Brand EU) is a pro-EU research and communications centre created and run by think tank [Gold Mercury International](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gold_Mercury_International) and CorporateVision.io a strategy consulting firm, both located in [London](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/London), [England](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/England), [United Kingdom](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Kingdom).

Brand extension or brand stretching is a [marketing strategy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing_strategy) in which a [firm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firm) marketing a product with a well-developed image uses the same [brand name](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_name) in a different product category.

brand implementation refers to the physical representation and consistent application of [brand identity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_identity) across [visual](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Visual_brand_language) and [verbal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_language) media.

categories: Branding companies of the United States

definition: Brand language is the body of words, phrases, and terms that an organization uses to describe its purpose or in reference to its products.

media.file: 2013-04-30 visual identity design of city branding of Amsterdam during the Coronation.jpg

definition: brand loyalty refers to a consumer's consistent preference for a particular brand and their commitment to repeatedly purchasing its products or services despite competing alternatives, market changes, or product shortcomings.
brand management refers to the process of controlling how a brand is perceived in the [market](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Market_(economics)).

Brand networking is the engagement of a [social networking](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_networking) service around a brand by providing consumers with a platform of relevant content, elements of participation, and a currency, score, or ranking.

Brand piracy is the act of naming a [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) in a manner which can result in confusion with other better known [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand)s.
brand preference represents which brands are preferred under assumptions of equality in price and availability.

Brand protection is the process and set of actions that a right holder undertakes to prevent third parties from using its [intellectual property](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Intellectual_property) without permission or impersonating the brand for fraudulent purposes.

consumer-brand relationship, also known as a brand relationship, is the relationship that [consumer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer)s think, feel, and have with a product or company [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand).
Brand strength analysis describes efforts to determine the strength a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) has compared with its [competitors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Competitors).
brand tribe is a concept in [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) that refers to ephemeral groups that enable connections among consumers sharing passions or interests.
Brand valuation is the process of estimating the total financial value of a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand).

brand-new [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business))s or [service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Service_(economics))s are created and promoted under a new [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand).

Brandable software is typically [software](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Software) created by one company for the purpose of allowing other companies to obtain [resell rights](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resell_rights) or giveaway rights to the software, change the [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) associated with it, and sell it as if it were their own.

Branded asset management refers to the implementation of [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) modifications and [life-cycle management](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_lifecycle_management) of branded assets.

Branded content (also known as branded entertainment) is a type of [entertainment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Entertainment) product made or otherwise funded by an [advertiser](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Advertising).
branding agency is a firm that specializes in creating and launching brands and [rebranding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rebranding).

Brandiose Studios, Inc., commonly referred to as Brandiose, is an American [branding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) firm based in [San Diego](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Diego), [California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/California).

definition: Callanetics exercise programme was created by [Callan Pinckney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Callan_Pinckney) in the early 1980s.

Branding companies of the United States

definition: Category management is a [retailing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retailing) and [purchasing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Purchasing) concept in which the range of products purchased by a business organization or sold by a [retailer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retailer) is broken down into discrete groups of similar or related products.
Audio branding is the use of distinctive sound elements to uniquely identify products and services to consumers.

page: Category:Branding companies of the United States

definition: certification mark is a type of [trademark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trademark) whereby a trader uses the mark to indicate the origin, material, mode of manufacture of products, mode of performance of services, quality, accuracy of other characteristics of products or services.

term: Category:Redirects from trade names of drugs
page: Category:Redirects from trade names of drugs

definition: sound trademark is a [non-conventional](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-conventional_trademark) [trademark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trademark) where [sound](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sound) is used to perform the trademark function of uniquely identifying the commercial origin of products or services.
Celebrity branding, also called celebrity endorsement, is a marketing approach where a well-known person is used to promote a product, service, or cause.
Cer-vit is a family of glass-ceramic materials that were invented by [Owens Illinois](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Owens_Illinois) in the mid-1960s.
challenger brand is a brand in an industry where it is neither the market leader nor a niche brand.

categories: Branding companies of the United States

definition: Co-branding is a [marketing strategy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing_strategy) that involves [strategic alliance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strategic_alliance) of multiple brand names jointly used on a single product or service.

COBRA (consumers' online brand related activities) is a theoretical framework related to understanding [consumer's behavioural engagement](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer_behaviour) with brands on [social media](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Social_media).

community of style is a [community](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Community) formed on the basis of attachment to a combination of [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) or [marque](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marque).
CorePower Yoga, based in [Denver, Colorado](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Denver,_Colorado), is the largest yoga studio brand in the United States with more than 220 locations across 23 states.

Corning Ware, also written CorningWare, is a brand of unique [glass-ceramic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glass-ceramic) ([Pyroceram](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pyroceram)) [cookware](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cookware) resistant to [thermal shock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thermal_shock) introduced in 1958 by Corning Glass Works (later [Corning Inc.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corning_Inc.)) in the United States.

corporate branding refers to the practice of promoting the [brand name](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) of a [corporate entity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corporate_entity), as opposed to specific [products](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) or [services](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Service_(economics)).
corporate identity or corporate image is the manner in which a [corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corporation), firm or business enterprise presents itself to the public.

Corporate political responsibility (CPR) is a [corporate responsibility](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corporate_responsibility) concept that emphasizes the political dimension of a [company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Company)'s actions.

Corporate social responsibility (CSR) refers to companies conducting their core operations in a responsible and sustainable way to create a positive corporate social impact. It is a form of international private business [self-regulation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industry_self-regulation), |publisher=Ethics-Based Marketing |ref= |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20141123234752/http://www.ethicsba

Cranberry glass or Gold Ruby glass is a red glass made by adding [gold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gold) [salts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Salts) or [colloidal gold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colloidal_gold) to molten glass.

Cristallo is a [glass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glass) that is totally clear (like [rock crystal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rock_crystal)), without the slight yellow or greenish color originating from [iron oxide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Iron_oxide) impurities.

definition: Customer experience (CX) refers to the cognitive, emotional, sensory, and behavioral responses of a customer during all stages of interaction with a product or service, including pre-purchase, consumption, and post-purchase.

customer switching or consumer switching describes It describes a person, who continually moves his/her patronage from one company to another and highlights the ignorance of many organizations, including credit card companies, who strive for customer acquisition regardless of [retention](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Customer_retention) rates.

Customer value maximization (CVM) is a real-time service model that, proponents say, goes beyond basic [customer relationship management](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Customer_relationship_management) (CRM) capabilities, identifying and capturing maximum potential from prospective and existing customers.

Daily Burn is a [health and fitness](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Health_and_fitness) company with a membership of approximately 2.5 million that provides workout and nutrition programs on a variety of web, mobile, and TV apps.

Digital branding is a [brand management](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_management) technique that uses a combination of [internet branding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet_branding) and [digital marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_marketing) to develop a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) over a range of digital venues, including [internet](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Internet)-based relationships, Sons|year=2007|isbn=978-0-470-05793-3}} [[Category:Digital marketing]] [[Category:Brand management]] [[Category:Types of branding]] cuahp4ama1rg5xfe54td87v4xymmxnj

definition: Double jeopardy is an empirical law in [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) where, with few exceptions, the lower-market-share brands in a market have both far fewer buyers in a time period and also lower [brand loyalty](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_loyalty).

logo flag (also called a branding flag or corporate flag) is a [flag](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flag) displaying the [logo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Logo) or [corporate identity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corporate_identity) of an [organization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Organization), usually on a plain background, often white or in the organization's primary color.

Dragontrail is an [alkali](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alkali)-[aluminosilicate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Aluminosilicate) sheet glass manufactured by [AGC Inc.](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/AGC_Inc.) It is engineered for a combination of thinness, lightness and damage-resistance, similarly to [Corning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corning_Inc.)'s proprietary [Gorilla Glass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gorilla_Glass).

DURAN is a brand name for the internationally defined [borosilicate glass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Borosilicate_glass) 3.3 (DIN ISO 3585) produced by the German company [http://www.duran-group.com/de/home.html DURAN Group GmbH] since 2005 under license from the [Schott AG](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schott_AG), which was the first to develop it, and which sold it from 1893 until the [equity carve-out](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Equity_carve-out) of the DURAN Group in 2005.
electronic registration mark is a proposed category of [trademark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trademark) that would restrict the use of trademarked words and phrases in online advertising.

categories: Branding companies of the United States

definition: Emotional branding is a term used within [marketing communication](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing_communication) that refers to the practice of building [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand)s that appeal directly to a [consumer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer)'s emotional state, needs and aspirations.
Employer brand is branding and marketing the entirety of the employment experience.

Enterprise cultural heritage (ECH) is the combination of history and products that belong to an enterprise and are recognised by the organization as a potential resource of novelty, innovation, and differentiation of products and services.
EPI (ethical positioning index) is an index which measures how [ethically](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ethics) a [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_name) is positioned.
Eucerin ( , ) is a trademarked brand of [Beiersdorf AG](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beiersdorf_AG).

European Glass Experience is a project coordinated by the City of [Venice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Venice) in collaboration with Consorzio Promovetro Murano and the [Murano Glass Museum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Murano_Glass_Museum).
FACOM (Fujitsu Automatic COMputer) is a [trademark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trademark) used for [Fujitsu](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fujitsu)'s computers.

flanker refers to newly created perfume that shares some attributes of an already existing perfume.

foreign branding is the use of foreign or foreign-sounding brand names for companies, goods, and services to imply they are of foreign origin, generally to make them appear to come from a place that seems attractively fitting, or at least exotic.

Foturan (notation of the manufacturer: FOTURAN) is a [photosensitive glass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Photosensitive_glass) by [SCHOTT Corporation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Schott_AG) developed in 1984.

GE multifactorial analysis is a technique used in [brand marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) and [product management](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_management) to help a company decide what [products](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) to add to its [portfolio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Portfolio_(finance)) and which opportunities in the market they should continue to invest in.

definition: graphic charter of government communication is the [graphic charter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graphic_charter) of the logo of [France](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/France), used by government services.

Green brands are those [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand)s that [consumer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer)s associate with [environmental conservation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Environmental_protection) and [sustainable business](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sustainable_business) practices.

hallmark is an official [mark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mark_(sign)) or series of marks struck on items made of [metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metal), mostly to certify the content of [noble metal](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Noble_metal)s—such as [platinum](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Platinum), [gold](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gold), [silver](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Silver) and in some nations, [palladium](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Palladium).

Hayes-Wheelwright Matrix, also known as the product-process matrix, is a [[tool#Non-material usage|tool]] used to analyze the fit between a chosen product positioning and the appropriate [manufacturing process](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manufacturing_process).
Hexoloy is a registered trademark for a pressureless sintered form of alpha silicon carbide.

definition: Jane Fonda's Workout, also known as Workout Starring Jane Fonda, is a 1982 exercise video by actress [Jane Fonda](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jane_Fonda), based on an exercise routine developed by Leni Cazden and refined by Cazden and Fonda at Workout, their exercise studio in Beverly Hills.

definition: John Gerzema is an American CEO and columnist who focuses on social sciences and the impact of leadership ethics and corporate culture on consumer behavior and financial performance.

Koh Buck Song (; born 1963) is a Singaporean writer, poet, and country brand adviser.

La Rochere is the oldest continuously working [glass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glass) factory in Europe located in the forests of the [Lorraine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lorraine) and [Franche-Comté](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Franche-Comté) regions that provided [firewood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Firewood) for [furnace](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Industrial_furnace)s and [fern](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fern)s, the ashes of which made the [potash](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Potash) necessary for the glass fusion.

definition: Lomography, or simply lomo, is a photographic style which involves taking spontaneous photographs with minimal attention to technical details.

Lumen is a [Milan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Milan)-based multi-disciplined creative and [design](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Design) [consulting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consultant) agency, founded by Pietro Rovatti and Drew Smith in 2003.

luxury good (or upmarket good) is a [product or item](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Good_(economics)) or [service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Service_(economics)) for which [demand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demand_(economics)) increases more than what is proportional as [income](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Income) rises, so that expenditures on the good become a more significant proportion of overall spending.

Major League Baseball logo was designed by [Jerry Dior](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jerry_Dior) in 1968 and was included on all on-field uniforms of [Major League Baseball](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Major_League_Baseball) (MLB) employees beginning in the [1969 season](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/1969_Major_League_Baseball_season).
Mandatory labelling or labeling (see [spelling differences](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spelling_differences)) is the requirement of consumer products to state their ingredients or components.
mark is a written or imprinted symbol used to indicate some trait of an item, for example, its ownership or maker.

Market cannibalization, market cannibalism, or corporate cannibalism is the practice of slashing the price of a product or introducing a new product into a market of established [product categories](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(category_theory)).

Marlan is a registered [trademark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trademark) of a permanently flame retardant [fabric](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Textiles) (certified according to the UNE EN 11612) used for [protective clothing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Personal_protective_equipment) for workers in [foundries](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foundry).

Marlboro Friday refers to April 2, 1993, when [Philip Morris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Philip_Morris_USA) announced a 20% price cut to their [Marlboro](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marlboro_(cigarette)) cigarettes to fight back against generic competitors, which were increasingly eating into their market share.

naming firm is a type of [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) service that specializes in the linguistic art and science of product and company [onomastics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Onomastics).

operator logo is a logo which appears on the status screen of a [mobile phone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mobile_phone).

planned obsolescence (also called built-in obsolescence or premature obsolescence) is the concept of policies planning or designing a [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Good_(economics)) with an artificially limited [useful life](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_lifetime) or a purposely frail design, so that it becomes [obsolete](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Obsolete) after a certain predetermined period of time upon which it decrementally functions or suddenly ceases to function, or might be perceived as [unfashionable](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fashion).

definition: product bundling is offering several [products](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) or services for sale as one combined product or service package.

Product churning is the business practice whereby more of the [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) is sold than is beneficial to the [consumer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer).
Product life-cycle management (PLM) is the succession of strategies by business management as a product goes through its [life-cycle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_lifecycle_management).

product lifecycle management (PLM) is the process of managing the entire lifecycle of a product from its inception through the [engineering](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_engineering), [design](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_design), and [manufacture](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manufacturing), as well as the service and disposal of manufactured products.
product lining refers to the offering of several related [products](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) for individual sale.

product naming is the discipline of deciding what a [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) will be called, and is very similar in concept and approach to the process of deciding on a name for a company or organization.

definition: Professionalism is a set of standards that an individual is expected to adhere to in a [workplace](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Workplace), usually in order to appear serious, uniform, or respectful.

Project portfolio management (PPM) is the centralized management of the processes, methods, and [technologies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Contract_management_software) used by [project manager](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_manager)s and [project management office](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Project_management_office)s (PMOs) to analyze and collectively manage current or proposed projects based on numerous key characteristics.

Promotional apparel is [clothing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clothing) imprinted with a [company](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Company)'s name, [logo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Logo) or message.

promotional model is a [model](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Model_(person)) hired to drive [consumer demand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Demand) for a product, service, brand, or concept by directly interacting with potential customers.

definition: Rebranding is a [marketing strategy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing_strategy) (often called relabeling) in which a new name, term, symbol, design, concept or combination thereof is created for an established [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand) with the intention of developing a new, differentiated identity in the minds of [consumer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Consumer)s, [investor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Investor)s, [competitor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Competition_(economics))s, and other [stakeholder](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stakeholder_(corporate))s.

Reusable packaging is manufactured of durable materials and is specifically designed for multiple trips and extended life.

definition: school brand is any type of term, mark or insignia, which distinguishes one school from another.
Sciros (or Pacific Rose) is a [cultivar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cultivar) of domesticated [apple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apple).

Seasonal packaging is a way of [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) a [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) and sparking sales in consumer segments that infrequently buy the product, by wrapping the product in a [package](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Packaging_and_labeling) closely related to seasons or holidays such as [Valentine's Day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Valentine's_Day), [Easter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Easter), [Mother's Day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mother's_Day), [Memorial Day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Memorial_Day), the [Fourth of July](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Independence_Day_(United_States)), [Labor Day](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Labor_Day), back-to-school, [Halloween](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Halloween), [Thanksgiving](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thanksgiving), [Christmas and New Year's](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christmas_and_holiday_season).
Self-branding describes the process in which consumers match their own [self-concept](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Self-concept) with the images of a certain brand.
Semantic Brand Score (SBS) is a measure of [brand importance](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_importance) that is calculated on textual data.
Sense Worldwide is a London-based [co-creation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Co-creation) consultancy.

definition: Sensory branding is a type of [marketing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing) that appeals to the [senses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Senses) in order to relate with customers on an emotional level.

definition: sigil of Baphomet is a [sigil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sigil) of the material world, representing carnality and earthly principles.

Status brands are [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand)s that, through association, inherently increase their owner's popularity in a certain community.

Super Slow is a form of strengthening physical exercise ([resistance training](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Strength_training)) popularized by [Ken Hutchins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ken_Hutchins).

definition: Verbal identity or verbal brand identity is the linguistic component of an organisation's [brand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand).

Vignelli Associates was a design firm co-founded and run by [Massimo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Massimo_Vignelli) and [Lella Vignelli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lella_Vignelli) in New York City, from 1971 to 2014.

Visions is a brand of transparent stove top [cookware](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cookware_and_bakeware) created by [Corning](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corning_Inc.) France and introduced to [Europe](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Europe) during the late 1970s.
Visual merchandising is the practice in the retail industry of optimizing the presentation of products and services to better highlight their features and benefits.

Vitrite is a very low [fusing point](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Melting_point) black [glass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Glass) mainly used for the [insulation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Insulator_(electrical)) base of [electric lamps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Incandescent_light_bulb).

Vula Bula is the [trademark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trademark) name of a number of openly licensed and representative reading resources, Vula Bula now belongs to Halala! Education, since Molteno closed down at the end of 2023.

Wallace Olins [CBE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CBE) (19 December 1930 – 14 April 2014) was a British practitioner of [corporate identity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Corporate_identity) and branding.
Waterford Crystal is an Irish manufacturer of [crystal glassware](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lead_glass), especially [cut glass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cut_glass) products.

categories: Branding companies of the United States

definition: white-label product is a [product](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Product_(business)) or service produced by one company (the [producer](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Production_(economics))) that other companies (the [marketers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Marketing)) [rebrand](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rebranding) to make it appear as if they had made it.
Whole-life cost is the [total cost of ownership](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Total_cost_of_ownership) over the life of an [asset](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asset).
WHP Global is an American [brand management](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brand_management) firm active in the retail industry.

definition: Wine labels are important sources of information for consumers since they tell the type and origin of the wine.

definition: Wood's glass is an [optical filter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Optical_filter) glass invented in 1903 by American physicist [Robert Williams Wood](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Williams_Wood) (1868 ==Health effects== Bulbs made of Wood's glass are potentially hazardous in comparison with those made of enameled glass, since the reduced visible light output may cause observers to be exposed to unsafe levels of UV, because the source appears dim.

definition: zombie trademark, orphan brand or zombie mark is an abandoned [trademark](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trademark) from a brand or company which is revived by a new enterprise with no affiliation to the former brand.
